No tyramine (migraine diet) – avoid aged cheese, cured meat, yeast extract. A fresh hamburger without cheese might be safe.
Low nickel diet – avoid whole grains, nuts, seeds, chocolate. Apple slices and plain hamburger (no bun?) Maybe white bun is okay.
I have mast cell activation syndrome – need low histamine, low tyramine, no leftovers. Fresh apple slices and fresh grilled chicken? Is the chicken fresh?
Low vitamin K diet (for blood thinners). Avoid green leafy vegetables. No spinach, kale. Your salad has lettuce (low in K), so side salad might be okay.

GAPS diet (gut healing) – no grains, no sugar, no processed foods. Bone broth would be great but you don't have it. Maybe plain grilled chicken?
SCD diet (Specific Carbohydrate Diet) – no complex carbs, no grains, no dairy except certain yogurts. Plain grilled chicken without bun.
Low microbial diet (for neutropenic patients) – no fresh fruits or vegetables (risk of bacteria). Cooked food only. Fries are cooked, so maybe fries and a well-done burger.
I'm on a clear liquid diet before a procedure – only clear liquids. Black coffee and water? That's it.
Full liquid diet – smoothie without chunks. Your strawberry banana smoothie has yogurt (liquid), but also maybe bits of fruit? Still, I'll try it.
I need a high calorie meal for weight gain – double quarter pounder with cheese and a shake.
Low calorie, low fat, low sodium – I guess I'm eating air. But maybe a side salad without dressing?
High protein, low carb, low fat – grilled chicken breast without bun, no sauce.
I'm trying to reduce oxalates – avoid spinach, nuts, wheat. A plain hamburger without bun? The bun has wheat, so maybe just the patty.
Low sulfur diet (for some digestive issues) – avoid eggs, dairy, meat. That's tough. Maybe apple slices and a salad without dressing?
I have PKU (phenylketonuria) – need low protein. Apple slices are fine, but no meat, no dairy. Any low protein item?
Low potassium diet (kidney disease) – avoid high potassium foods like potatoes, bananas, oranges. French fries are high in potassium, so maybe a hamburger without fries?
Low phosphorus diet (kidney disease) – avoid dairy, nuts, whole grains. A plain hamburger without cheese might work.
I'm following the MIND diet (for brain health) – lots of berries, leafy greens, nuts. You have apple slices (not berries) and side salad (lettuce). Could be okay.
Flexitarian vegan (mostly vegan but sometimes eggs?) No, I'll stick to vegan. Apple slices and black coffee.
I'm on a no-sugar, no-flour diet (like Whole30 but without the meat restrictions). Grilled chicken without bun?
Low acid diet (for GERD) – avoid acidic foods like tomatoes, citrus, soda. Water is fine, maybe a plain hamburger without ketchup?
I have a nickel allergy (dietary nickel) – avoid whole grains, nuts, seeds, chocolate. A hamburger on a white bun might be low nickel.
Low lectin diet (plant toxins) – avoid beans, grains, nightshades. No potatoes, no tomatoes, no wheat. That's most of the menu. Apple slices maybe.
